marketFocus: Iran conflict escalates over weekend: Houthis fire missiles at Israel for the first time, US-Israel strike Iran's largest steel plants + energy facilities. US bases attacked in Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait, Iraq. WTI briefly above $103/bbl, Brent above $116/bbl at open.

Trump mixed signals: 'good negotiations with Iran' + 'could take Kharg Island very easily.' Iran gave 20 boats of oil passage but Hormuz remains effectively closed. Trump policy meeting at 18:30 FFT — key catalyst for risk sentiment direction.

German CPI Prelim at 13:00 FFT is today's key data event — hot print reinforces ECB two-hike narrative. Swiss KOF Leading Indicators missed badly (96.1 vs 102 exp). BoJ Summary of Opinions hawkish: debated need for more rate hikes at March meeting.

USD.tone: DXY Firmer on Haven + Stagflation — Trump Policy Meeting at 18:30 FFT Key
USD.summary: USD is Bullish today. DXY opened the week on a firmer footing as risk aversion carried over from the weekend escalation — Houthis joined the Iran conflict, US-Israel struck steel plants and energy facilities, and US bases were attacked across the region. The greenback's major counterparts are mixed but the dollar retains its reserve, haven, and yield premium. Fed Williams comments today are the key CB catalyst. German CPI Prelim at 13:00 FFT and Trump's policy meeting at 18:30 FFT are the key market-moving events. Weekly Strong Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: DXY firmer as risk aversion from weekend escalation carries into new week
Haven + stagflation framework: oil above $100/bbl feeds inflation + Fed hike repricing
Houthis enter conflict for first time — Bab al-Mandab Strait risk now added to Hormuz
Fed Williams comments today — key CB catalyst for USD direction
Trump policy meeting at 18:30 FFT — key catalyst for risk sentiment and USD

EUR.tone: ECB Hike Repricing Intact — German CPI Prelim at 13:00 FFT the Key Catalyst
EUR.summary: EUR is Bullish today. EUR/USD recovered from an early dip beneath 1.1500 to return to relatively flat territory — the ECB hawkish repricing story remains the dominant driver. ECB Villeroy said 'ECB is ready to act, but too early to discuss dates for possible rate hikes' — a measured hawkish tone. German CPI Prelim at 13:00 FFT is the key event: a hot print (above 2.5% YoY) would reinforce the two-hike narrative and push EUR higher. ECB Schnabel on Friday said 'no need to rush' but markets are not listening. Weekly Weak Bullish + Daily Bullish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: EUR/USD recovered from sub-1.1500 dip — ECB hawkish repricing dominates
ECB Villeroy: 'ECB is ready to act' — measured hawkish tone intact
German CPI Prelim at 13:00 FFT — hot print reinforces ECB two-hike narrative
Markets price ~85bps of ECB tightening by year-end; rate-cut expectations removed
Energy importer headwind offset by rates repricing story — EUR holding up in risk-off

GBP.tone: UK Clocks Switch to BST — Quiet Newsflow, BOE Repricing Intact but No Catalyst
GBP.summary: GBP is Neutral today. UK clocks switched from GMT to BST on Sunday — the NY-London differential returned to its usual 5 hours. UK-specific newsflow is quiet with no major data releases today. UK Lloyds Business Barometer beat (55 vs 44 prev) but failed to spur any FX reaction. GBP/USD marginally rebounded from last week's trough at sub-1.3300. The BOE repricing story (~81bps of tightening priced) remains intact but needs fresh catalyst to drive GBP. Weekly Neutral + Daily Neutral = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: UK clocks switch to BST — NY-London differential returns to 5 hours
UK Lloyds Business Barometer beat (55 vs 44 prev) — no FX reaction
GBP/USD marginally rebounded from sub-1.3300 — quiet UK newsflow
BOE repricing (~81bps tightening priced) intact but needs fresh catalyst
No major UK data today — GBP trading on global risk sentiment and oil prices

JPY.tone: Verbal Intervention Jawboning — USD/JPY Trickled Lower but Energy Vulnerability Intact
JPY.summary: JPY is Bearish today. USD/JPY trickled lower following jawboning from Japan's top FX diplomat Mimura ('bold action may be needed if the Middle East situation continues; targeting all fronts in market for action') and BoJ Governor Ueda ('will scrutinise how FX moves affect growth and price forecasts'). BoJ Summary of Opinions from March meeting was hawkish — debated need for more rate hikes, some members signalled need to accelerate increases. However, Japan imports 90%+ of crude from the Middle East — the Hormuz closure and Houthis entering the conflict is a direct economic shock. Verbal intervention is not credible without coordinated action. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bearish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: Japan's FX diplomat Mimura: 'bold action may be needed' — verbal intervention jawboning
BoJ Summary of Opinions hawkish: debated more rate hikes, some members want to accelerate
BoJ Ueda: 'will scrutinise how FX moves affect growth and prices'
Japan imports 90%+ of crude from Middle East — Hormuz + Houthis = direct economic shock
USD/JPY trickled lower on jawboning but energy vulnerability limits JPY recovery

AUD.tone: Diesel Shortage Risk + Risk-Off + PBoC Weaker Yuan Fix — AUD Under Pressure
AUD.summary: AUD is Bearish today. APAC stocks were pressured following the weekend escalation — ASX 200 declined with underperformance in tech and financials. Australia faces heightened diesel shortage risk: 90% import dependent, stock levels at 1-5 weeks cover (well below international benchmarks). Australia's national cabinet meeting to discuss oil price situation — Treasurer Chalmers has not ruled out cutting fuel taxes. PBoC set a weaker yuan fix (6.9223 vs exp 6.9205) — bearish for antipodeans. Westpac now sees RBA terminal rate at 4.85% (was 4.10%) — medium-term positive but cannot offset immediate risk-off. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Australia diesel shortage risk: 90% import dependent, 1-5 weeks cover only
ASX 200 lower — tech and financials underperforming; energy sector cushioning losses
PBoC set weaker yuan fix (6.9223 vs exp 6.9205) — bearish for antipodeans
Westpac upgrades RBA terminal rate to 4.85% (from 4.10%) — medium-term positive only
Australia national cabinet meeting on oil price situation — fuel tax cut under consideration

CAD.tone: WTI Above $103/bbl + Houthis Enter Conflict — Maximum Petro-Proxy Support
CAD.summary: CAD is Bullish today. WTI briefly climbed above $103/bbl and Brent above $116/bbl at the open following the weekend escalation — Houthis fired missiles at Israel for the first time, widening the risk to the Bab al-Mandab Strait (another key oil shipping route). Egypt is now importing Libyan oil to replace Kuwait supply disrupted by Hormuz closure — the global oil supply chain disruption is intensifying. SocGen sees growing likelihood of Brent topping $150/bbl. No major CAD data today — pure oil proxy. Weekly Neutral + Daily Bullish = NO High Conviction alignment.
drivers: WTI briefly above $103/bbl, Brent above $116/bbl — Houthis enter conflict for first time
Houthis entering conflict widens risk to Bab al-Mandab Strait — second key oil route
Egypt importing Libyan oil to replace Kuwait supply — global supply chain disruption
SocGen: growing likelihood of Brent topping $150/bbl; Brent may average $125/bbl in April
No major CAD data today — pure oil proxy, tracks Brent directly

CHF.tone: KOF Leading Indicators Miss + Haven Flows to USD — CHF Underperforming
CHF.summary: CHF is Bearish today. Swiss KOF Leading Indicators for March missed badly (96.1 vs 102 exp, prev 104.2) — a significant deterioration in the Swiss economic outlook. Haven flows continue to favour USD over CHF due to the yield advantage. Fitch affirmed Switzerland at AAA with Stable outlook — no surprise, no CHF reaction. SNB intervention cap remains intact, limiting appreciation. Spanish Retail Sales missed (YoY 2.2% vs 3.8% exp) — weak European data adds to the risk-off tone without supporting CHF specifically. Weekly Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: Swiss KOF Leading Indicators missed badly: 96.1 vs 102 exp (prev 104.2) — growth concerns
Haven flows favour USD over CHF — yield advantage keeps dollar preferred
Fitch affirmed Switzerland AAA Stable — no surprise, no CHF reaction
SNB intervention cap limits appreciation; sight deposits rose modestly last week
Spanish Retail Sales missed (YoY 2.2% vs 3.8% exp) — weak European data, no CHF support

NZD.tone: Antipodeans Subdued — PBoC Weaker Fix + Risk-Off + No Domestic Catalyst
NZD.summary: NZD is Bearish today. Antipodeans were subdued amid the downbeat risk appetite following the weekend escalation. PBoC set a weaker yuan fix (6.9223 vs exp 6.9205) — directly bearish for NZD as China is New Zealand's largest trading partner. China-linked ships (Cosco Shipping) were reported attempting to cross the Strait of Hormuz — a reminder of China's exposure to the conflict. No domestic NZD catalyst today. High-beta risk proxy in a risk-off environment with no RBNZ support until April. Weekly Weak Bearish + Daily Bearish = HIGH CONVICTION alignment ✅.
drivers: PBoC set weaker yuan fix (6.9223 vs exp 6.9205) — directly bearish for NZD
China-linked Cosco ships attempting Hormuz crossing — China exposure reminder
Antipodeans subdued amid risk-off; high-beta with no domestic catalyst
No RBNZ support until April — policy neutral near-term
NZ consumer confidence at 91.3 (from 100.1) — pessimistic territory, no recovery signal
